title: moquilea bothynophylla
intro: Moquilea bothynophylla, also known as the "False Quassia" or "Peruvian Quassia", is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The medicinal properties of Moquilea bothynophylla include its ability to act as a natural insecticide and antimicrobial agent, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of using Moquilea bothynophylla include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of Moquilea bothynophylla can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
